description_long: This is a large, multi-story brick house built on a sloped terrain, with a combination of gable and flat roofs. The front features a driveway leading to a garage, a main entry door, and a small landscaped area. The rear of the house is elevated on stilts, providing a covered space beneath and two levels of balconies with railings. Windows are arranged in symmetrical rows, with some decorative trim and a mix of single and double window groupings. The house is surrounded by a low retaining wall and stone-paved walkways, with the terrain dropping off steeply at the back. The overall design blends traditional and modern elements, with a prominent use of brick and concrete.
description: A large, multi-story brick house with gable and flat roofs, built on a slope with balconies and a garage. The design features symmetrical windows, a landscaped front, and a rear supported by stilts.
description_view_0: Front view showing the garage, main entry door, small landscaped area, and paved driveway.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the brick facade, symmetrical windows, and the transition from the front yard to the sloped rear.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the elevated section on stilts, two levels of balconies, and the steep drop-off of the terrain.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the length of the house, window arrangement, and the supporting stilts at the back.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows with some grouped windows
attribute_entryway_design: Main entry with a single door, small porch, steps, and adjacent landscaped area
